machine.name: Power Cube Frame
machine.description: Build the steel frame and install core modules of the Power Cube hydraulic power unit. Hour 1 covers measuring, cutting, and welding the 4×4 tubular steel frame. Hour 2 covers mounting the engine plate, installing the engine, hydraulic pump and coupling, reservoir, pressure and return hoses, and oil cooler. By the end you will have a complete Power Cube ready for hydraulic tool connections.
learningObjectives: Read a cut list and mark steel tubing to correct lengths
Identify which tubes form the base vs. vertical posts
Lay out a rectangular base frame from four tubes
Tack-weld corners before committing to full welds
Use a framing square to verify 90° corners
Full-weld all base joints for structural integrity
Position vertical posts at each corner of the base
Complete the top ring to form the cube frame
Verify the finished frame is square and plumb
Cut and weld an engine mounting plate to the frame top
Align and torque an engine to its mounting plate
Install a pump coupling and mount a hydraulic gear pump
Mount a hydraulic reservoir and plumb pressure and return lines
Install an oil cooler and verify all fittings are leak-free
prerequisiteNotes: Familiarity with basic steel fabrication terms (tack weld, full weld, tube stock).
Access to an angle grinder or cutting torch for steel cutting.
Comfort using a MIG or stick welder.

sourceReferences.title: Power Cube — OSE Wiki
sourceReferences.notes: Primary reference for frame dimensions and assembly order.

sourceReferences.title: Power Cube VII Bill of Materials

assemblies.name: Power Cube Frame Assembly
assemblies.description: Full cube frame from cut steel tubing — base rectangle, vertical posts, and top ring.
assemblies.learningFocus: Steel cutting, layout, tack-welding, squareness checks, full welding, engine and pump mounting, hydraulic plumbing, and torque specifications.

subassemblies.name: Base Frame Rectangle
subassemblies.description: Four tubes forming the flat bottom rectangle of the Power Cube frame.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Base frame rectangle is welded and square. Ready for vertical posts!

subassemblies.name: Vertical Corner Posts
subassemblies.description: Four vertical tubes at each corner of the base rectangle.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: All four vertical posts are welded to the base. Almost there!

subassemblies.name: Top Ring
subassemblies.description: Four tubes forming the top rectangle, completing the cube frame.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: The Power Cube frame is complete! All 12 tubes are welded into a rigid cube.

subassemblies.name: Engine Mounting
subassemblies.description: Steel mounting plate welded to frame top and engine bolted to it.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Engine is mounted and torqued to spec. Ready for the hydraulic drive!

subassemblies.name: Hydraulic Pump & Coupling
subassemblies.description: Spider coupling connects engine output shaft to a 2-section hydraulic gear pump.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Hydraulic pump is coupled to the engine and torqued. Time to plumb the hydraulics!

subassemblies.name: Reservoir, Hoses & Cooler
subassemblies.description: Hydraulic oil reservoir, pressure and return hoses, and oil cooler complete the hydraulic circuit.
subassemblies.milestoneMessage: Hydraulic circuit is complete! The Power Cube is ready for tool connections.

parts.name: Base Long Tube 1
parts.function: Front rail of the base frame rectangle.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Lay flat with the cut ends facing left and right.

parts.name: Base Long Tube 2
parts.function: Back rail of the base frame rectangle.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Lay flat, parallel to the front tube.

parts.name: Base Short Tube 1
parts.function: Left side of the base frame rectangle.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Lay flat, connecting front and back tubes on the left.

parts.name: Base Short Tube 2
parts.function: Right side of the base frame rectangle.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Lay flat, connecting front and back tubes on the right.

parts.name: Vertical Post 1
parts.function: Front-left vertical corner post.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Stand upright on the base corner.

parts.name: Vertical Post 2
parts.function: Front-right vertical corner post.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Stand upright on the base corner.

parts.name: Vertical Post 3
parts.function: Back-left vertical corner post.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Stand upright on the base corner.

parts.name: Vertical Post 4
parts.function: Back-right vertical corner post.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Stand upright on the base corner.

parts.name: Top Long Tube 1
parts.function: Front rail of the top ring.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Horizontal across the tops of the front vertical posts.

parts.name: Top Long Tube 2
parts.function: Back rail of the top ring.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Horizontal across the tops of the back vertical posts.

parts.name: Top Short Tube 1
parts.function: Left side of the top ring.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Connects front and back top tubes on the left.

parts.name: Top Short Tube 2
parts.function: Right side of the top ring.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Connects front and back top tubes on the right.

parts.name: Engine Mounting Plate
parts.function: Provides a flat mounting surface for the engine on top of the frame.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Lay flat across the top of the frame, centered.

parts.name: Engine
parts.material: Cast iron block, aluminum head, steel mounting feet
parts.function: Provides mechanical power to drive the hydraulic pump via the coupling.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Output shaft faces the pump side of the frame. Mounting feet sit on the plate.

parts.name: Pump Coupling
parts.material: Cast iron hubs with polyurethane spider insert
parts.function: Connects the engine output shaft to the hydraulic pump input shaft with flexible vibration damping.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Slide one hub onto the engine shaft, the other onto the pump shaft, with the spider between.

parts.name: Hydraulic Pump
parts.material: Cast iron body, hardened steel gears
parts.function: Converts engine rotational power into hydraulic fluid flow and pressure.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Input shaft aligns with coupling. Outlet ports face outward for hose access.

parts.name: Hydraulic Reservoir
parts.material: Welded steel tank with internal baffles
parts.function: Stores hydraulic oil, allows air separation, and provides suction head for the pump.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Fill cap on top, drain plug at bottom. Suction port faces the pump.

parts.name: Pressure Hose
parts.material: SAE 100R2 steel-braided hose, 3/4" ID, rated 3000 PSI
parts.function: Carries pressurized hydraulic fluid from the pump outlet to the quick couplers.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Connects pump outlet port to the pressure manifold.

parts.name: Return Hose
parts.material: SAE 100R1 rubber hose, 1" ID, rated 1000 PSI
parts.function: Returns low-pressure hydraulic fluid from the tools back to the reservoir.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Connects return manifold to the reservoir return port.

parts.name: Oil Cooler
parts.function: Cools hydraulic oil to prevent overheating during sustained operation.
parts.defaultOrientationHint: Mount vertically on the frame side with fins exposed to airflow.

tools.name: Tape Measure
tools.purpose: Measures and marks steel tubing to the cut list dimensions.
tools.usageNotes: Hook the end on the tube edge, pull taut, mark with soapstone at the target length.

tools.name: Angle Grinder
tools.purpose: Cuts 4×4 tubular steel to length using a cutting disc.
tools.usageNotes: Wear eye and ear protection. Clamp the tube securely before cutting. Cut on the waste side of the mark.
tools.safetyNotes: Always wear safety glasses, face shield, and hearing protection. Keep the guard in place.

tools.name: MIG Welder
tools.purpose: Joins steel tubes at frame joints using MIG welding.
tools.usageNotes: Tack-weld corners first to hold geometry, then run full beads after squareness is confirmed.
tools.safetyNotes: Wear welding helmet (shade 10+), leather gloves, and long sleeves. Ensure adequate ventilation.

tools.name: Framing Square
tools.purpose: Verifies corners are at exactly 90 degrees.
tools.usageNotes: Place the square against both tubes at a corner. Both edges should make full contact with no gaps.

tools.name: C-Clamp
tools.purpose: Holds tubes in alignment during tack-welding.
tools.usageNotes: Clamp tubes flush before tacking. Remove clamps only after tack welds have cooled.

tools.name: Torque Wrench
tools.purpose: Tightens bolts to a precise torque specification to prevent under- or over-tightening.
tools.usageNotes: Set the desired torque on the micrometer scale. Pull smoothly until the wrench clicks — stop immediately at the click. Always torque in a star pattern.
tools.safetyNotes: Never use a torque wrench to loosen bolts. Store at the lowest torque setting to preserve calibration.

tools.name: Line Wrench (Flare-Nut)
tools.purpose: Tightens hydraulic hose fittings without rounding the soft brass or steel hex nuts.
tools.usageNotes: Slide over the hose, seat fully on the fitting hex, and turn 1/4 past finger-tight. Check for leaks before applying pressure.
tools.safetyNotes: Never tighten fittings while the system is pressurized. Wipe up any spilled hydraulic fluid immediately — it is slippery.

steps.name: Measure the long base tubes
steps.instructionText: Use the tape measure to mark two lengths of 4×4 tubular steel at 24 inches each. These will form the front and back rails of the base frame. Hook the tape on one end and mark at 24" with soapstone.
steps.whyItMattersText: Accurate measurements prevent misalignment when the frame is welded together. Even 1/8" off compounds at every joint.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Long tube lengths marked at 24 inches.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Use the tape measure on the long tube marking point.

steps.name: Cut the long base tubes
steps.instructionText: Equip the angle grinder and cut both long tubes at the marked lines. Clamp each tube to the worktable before cutting. Cut on the waste side of the line.
steps.whyItMattersText: Clean, straight cuts ensure the tube ends mate flush for strong welds. Cutting on the waste side preserves the marked dimension.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Front base tube cut to length.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the angle grinder and cut at the marked line on the front tube.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Back base tube cut to length.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Cut the second long tube at the marked line.

steps.name: Measure the short base tubes
steps.instructionText: Measure and mark two more tubes at 16 inches each. These shorter tubes form the left and right sides of the base rectangle.
steps.whyItMattersText: The short sides set the width of the frame. The Power Cube's square-ish footprint depends on these being precisely matched.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Short tube lengths marked at 16 inches.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Use the tape measure on the short tube marking point.

steps.name: Cut the short base tubes
steps.instructionText: Cut both short tubes at the marked 16-inch lines using the angle grinder. Same technique: clamp, cut on waste side, deburr the ends.
steps.whyItMattersText: Both short tubes must be identical length so the frame sits flat and the corners are square.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Left base tube cut to length.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the angle grinder and cut the left short tube.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Right base tube cut to length.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Cut the second short tube.

steps.name: Lay out the base rectangle
steps.instructionText: Arrange the four cut tubes on a flat surface to form a rectangle. The two long tubes (24") are front and back, the two short tubes (16") connect them on left and right. Butt the short tube ends against the inside faces of the long tubes.
steps.whyItMattersText: Layout determines the final frame shape. Getting the rectangle right before tacking saves hours of rework.

steps.name: Tack-weld the base corners
steps.instructionText: Equip the welder. Place a small tack weld (about 1/2 inch) at each of the four corners where the tubes meet. Tack welds hold the geometry while you verify squareness — don't run full beads yet.
steps.whyItMattersText: Tack welds are easy to break and redo. Full welds are permanent. Always tack first, check square, then commit.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Front-left corner tacked.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the welder and tack the front-left corner.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Front-right corner tacked.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Tack the front-right corner.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Back-left corner tacked.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Tack the back-left corner.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Back-right corner tacked.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Tack the back-right corner.

steps.name: Check the base for square
steps.instructionText: Use the framing square at each corner to verify 90° angles. Also measure both diagonals — they should be equal. If the frame is racked, break a tack weld, adjust, and re-tack.
steps.whyItMattersText: A frame that's even 1° out of square will cause every subsequent module to fit poorly. This is the critical quality gate.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Base frame is square! Diagonals match within 1/16".
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Place the framing square against a corner to check for 90°.

steps.name: Full-weld the base frame
steps.instructionText: Now that the base is confirmed square, run full weld beads on all four corners. Weld both sides of each joint for maximum strength. Let each weld cool before moving to the next to minimize warping.
steps.whyItMattersText: Full welds provide the structural strength to support the engine, pump, and hydraulic modules. Tack welds alone would fail under vibration.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: All four base corners fully welded. Solid foundation!
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the welder and weld all four base corners.

steps.name: Cut the four vertical posts
steps.instructionText: Measure and cut four vertical posts at 20 inches each from 4×4 tubular steel. These posts will stand upright at each corner of the base.
steps.whyItMattersText: The vertical posts define the cube height. All four must be identical so the top ring sits level.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: All four vertical posts cut to 20 inches.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Use the angle grinder to cut the vertical post stock.

steps.name: Stand up the vertical posts
steps.instructionText: Place each vertical post upright at its base corner. The post should sit on top of the base tube intersection. Use clamps to hold each post plumb while you prepare to weld.
steps.whyItMattersText: Posts must be perfectly vertical (plumb) or the top ring won't sit flat and the cube will be racked.

steps.name: Weld the vertical posts to the base
steps.instructionText: Tack-weld each post to the base first, check plumb with the framing square, then run full welds around each post base. Weld on opposite sides alternately to reduce heat distortion.
steps.whyItMattersText: The vertical post welds carry the weight of the engine and all top-mounted modules. They must be solid and plumb.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: All four vertical posts welded to the base.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the welder and weld each vertical post to the base.

steps.name: Place the top ring tubes
steps.instructionText: Place the four top ring tubes across the tops of the vertical posts, forming the same rectangle pattern as the base. Long tubes front and back, short tubes left and right.
steps.whyItMattersText: The top ring locks the vertical posts together and creates the mounting surface for the engine plate and pump module.

steps.name: Weld the top ring
steps.instructionText: Tack-weld the top ring corners first, verify squareness and plumb one last time, then full-weld all top joints. The cube frame is almost complete.
steps.whyItMattersText: These final welds close the cube structure, making it rigid in all three dimensions.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Top ring fully welded!
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the welder and weld the top ring corners.

steps.name: Final squareness and plumb check
steps.instructionText: Use the framing square and tape measure to do a final quality inspection. Check corners for 90°, measure diagonals on all six faces, and verify the frame sits flat. Congratulations — the Power Cube frame is complete!
steps.whyItMattersText: This final check ensures the frame will accept all modules (engine, pump, reservoir, cooler) without shimming or rework.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Frame passes inspection! Square, plumb, and ready for module installation.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Use the framing square to do the final inspection.

steps.name: Cut the engine mounting plate
steps.instructionText: Measure and cut a 1/4" steel plate to fit across the top of the frame. The plate should span the full width and depth of the top ring, resting on the top tubes.
steps.whyItMattersText: The mounting plate distributes the engine weight across all four top tubes. A plate that's too small concentrates stress and can crack welds.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Mounting plate cut to size.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Use the angle grinder to cut the mounting plate to size.

steps.name: Place the mounting plate on the frame
steps.instructionText: Set the cut plate flat on top of the frame, centered so it rests evenly on all four top tubes. The plate edges should be flush with or slightly inside the outer faces of the tubes.
steps.whyItMattersText: Centering the plate ensures the engine sits balanced on the frame and that weld access is equal on all sides.

steps.name: Weld the mounting plate to the frame
steps.instructionText: Tack-weld the plate at each corner first, verify it's still centered, then run full fillet welds along all four edges where the plate meets the top tubes.
steps.whyItMattersText: Full perimeter welds provide maximum strength. The plate must support engine vibration, so tacks alone would crack.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Mounting plate fully welded to frame.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Equip the welder and weld the plate at all four corners.

steps.name: Place the engine on the mounting plate
steps.instructionText: Lift the engine onto the mounting plate. Orient it so the output shaft faces the pump side of the frame (left side). Align the engine mounting feet with the bolt holes in the plate.
steps.whyItMattersText: Correct shaft orientation is critical — the pump coupling requires precise alignment. Getting it right now prevents having to unbolt and reposition later.

steps.name: Torque the engine mounting bolts
steps.instructionText: Insert bolts through the engine feet and plate. Torque all four bolts to 35 ft-lbs in a star pattern: front-left, back-right, front-right, back-left.
steps.whyItMattersText: Star-pattern torquing ensures the engine seats evenly. Under-torqued bolts vibrate loose; over-torqued bolts strip threads or warp the plate.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: All engine bolts torqued to 35 ft-lbs.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Use the torque wrench on the engine mounting bolts.

steps.name: Install the pump coupling
steps.instructionText: Slide one coupling hub onto the engine output shaft and secure it with the set screw. Place the spider insert into the hub jaws. The second hub will engage when the pump is mounted.
steps.whyItMattersText: The spider coupling absorbs vibration and minor misalignment. Without it, shaft-to-shaft contact would transmit destructive vibrations to the pump bearings.

steps.name: Mount the hydraulic pump
steps.instructionText: Position the hydraulic pump so its input shaft engages the second coupling hub. The pump body should rest on the mounting bracket welded to the frame side. Slide the pump shaft into the coupling jaws.
steps.whyItMattersText: Precise shaft-to-coupling alignment prevents premature bearing failure. The pump must be supported by its bracket — never hanging from the coupling alone.

steps.name: Torque the pump mounting bolts
steps.instructionText: Torque the pump bracket bolts to 25 ft-lbs. Check that the pump shaft spins freely by hand after torquing — if it binds, loosen and realign.
steps.whyItMattersText: Over-tightening can distort the pump body and bind the gears. The free-spin check confirms alignment is correct.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Pump bolts torqued. Shaft spins freely — alignment is good!
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Use the torque wrench on the pump mounting bolts.

steps.name: Mount the hydraulic reservoir
steps.instructionText: Position the reservoir on the right side of the frame. The suction port should face the pump, and the fill cap must be accessible from above. Secure it to the frame with the mounting straps.
steps.whyItMattersText: The reservoir must be higher than the pump suction inlet to maintain positive suction head. Gravity-feeding prevents pump cavitation.

steps.name: Torque the reservoir mounting bolts
steps.instructionText: Torque the reservoir strap bolts to 20 ft-lbs. The tank should be snug but not deformed — the straps should grip without crushing the tank walls.
steps.whyItMattersText: The reservoir holds 10 gallons of oil (about 75 lbs). Loose straps let it shift under vibration; overtight straps can dent the tank and cause leaks.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: Reservoir secured. Tank is snug and level.
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Use the torque wrench on the reservoir mounting straps.

steps.name: Connect the pressure hose
steps.instructionText: Route the high-pressure hose from the pump outlet port to the pressure manifold. Avoid sharp bends — maintain at least a 4-inch bend radius. Thread the fittings finger-tight for now.
steps.whyItMattersText: The pressure line carries 2500+ PSI. Sharp bends weaken the hose, and a blown pressure line is extremely dangerous.

steps.name: Connect the return hose
steps.instructionText: Route the return hose from the return manifold back to the reservoir return port. The return line is lower pressure than the pressure line but still needs proper routing without kinks.
steps.whyItMattersText: A restricted return line causes back-pressure, overheating, and potential seal failures in the hydraulic motors.

steps.name: Mount the oil cooler
steps.instructionText: Bolt the oil cooler to the front face of the frame, oriented vertically for maximum airflow. Connect the cooler inlet hose from the return line tee and the cooler outlet to the reservoir.
steps.whyItMattersText: Hydraulic oil above 180°F degrades rapidly. The cooler keeps oil temperature in the safe range during sustained operation like brick pressing.

steps.name: Tighten all fittings and system check
steps.instructionText: Go back through every hydraulic fitting and tighten with the line wrench — 1/4 turn past finger-tight. Check that all hoses are routed clear of the engine exhaust and moving parts. The Power Cube is complete!
steps.whyItMattersText: A single loose fitting under 2500 PSI creates a dangerous injection hazard. This final pass catches any fittings that were left finger-tight during assembly.

requiredToolActions.successMessage: All fittings tight. The Power Cube is ready for first start-up!
requiredToolActions.failureMessage: Use the line wrench to tighten all hydraulic fittings.

validationRules.failureMessage: Place the front long tube at the front of the base rectangle.
Place the back long tube parallel to the front tube.
Place the left short tube connecting the front and back tubes.
Place the right short tube connecting the front and back tubes.
Stand the front-left post upright at the corner.
Stand the front-right post upright at the corner.
Stand the back-left post upright at the corner.
Stand the back-right post upright at the corner.
Place the front top tube across the two front posts.
Place the back top tube across the two back posts.
Place the left top tube connecting front and back top tubes.
Place the right top tube connecting front and back top tubes.
Center the mounting plate flat across the top of the frame.
Place the engine on the plate with the output shaft facing the pump side.
Slide the coupling onto the engine output shaft.
Mount the pump with its shaft engaging the coupling.
Mount the reservoir on the right side of the frame with the suction port facing the pump.
Route the pressure hose from the pump outlet to the pressure manifold.
Route the return hose from the return manifold to the reservoir.
Mount the oil cooler on the front face of the frame.

hints.title: Measuring technique
hints.message: Hook the tape on the tube end, pull taut to 24 inches, and mark with soapstone. Double-check by measuring from the mark back to the end.

hints.title: Cutting safety
hints.message: Clamp the tube to a stable surface. Wear safety glasses and hearing protection. Let the grinder do the work — don't force it through the steel.

hints.title: Base rectangle layout
hints.message: Long tubes go front and back. Short tubes butt against the inside faces of the long tubes on left and right. The result should be a flat rectangle on your work surface.

hints.title: Tack welding tips
hints.message: A tack weld is a short (1/2 inch) spot weld that holds parts in position. Do one tack per corner, alternating opposite corners (front-left, then back-right) to distribute heat evenly.

hints.title: Diagonal check for squareness
hints.message: Measure corner-to-corner diagonally in both directions. If the two diagonals are equal (within 1/16"), the frame is square. If not, push the long diagonal shorter and re-tack.

hints.title: Manage heat distortion
hints.message: Weld opposite corners in sequence (front-left, then back-right, then front-right, then back-left). This distributes heat and minimizes frame warping.

hints.title: Keep posts plumb
hints.message: Hold the framing square against the post and the base tube. The post should be perfectly vertical in both directions. Clamp it in place before tacking.

hints.title: Alternate welding sides
hints.message: When welding around a post base, weld one side, skip to the opposite side, then fill in the remaining two sides. This prevents heat from pulling the post out of plumb.

hints.title: Top ring matches base
hints.message: The top ring is the same rectangle as the base — same tube lengths, same orientation. Long tubes front/back, short tubes left/right.

hints.title: Plate sizing
hints.message: The plate should span the full top ring area. Measure inside the outer tube faces and cut 1/8" undersize for easy fitment. You can always weld over small gaps.

hints.title: Center the mounting plate
hints.message: The plate should sit evenly on all four top tubes. Check that the overhang is equal on all sides — use the tape measure to verify.

hints.title: Plate welding order
hints.message: Tack opposite corners first (front-left, then back-right), check centering, then run full beads. Weld opposite edges alternately to minimize warping.

hints.title: Engine shaft direction
hints.message: The engine output shaft must face the left side of the frame where the pump will mount. Look for the keyway on the shaft — that's the output end.

hints.title: Star-pattern torquing
hints.message: Torque bolts in a cross pattern: front-left → back-right → front-right → back-left. This seats the engine evenly and prevents the plate from flexing.

hints.title: Coupling installation
hints.message: Push the hub onto the shaft until the set screw aligns with the shaft flat. Tighten the set screw. Insert the spider — it should fit snugly in the hub jaws with no force.

hints.title: Pump alignment
hints.message: The pump shaft should slide into the coupling hub smoothly. If you have to force it, the shafts are misaligned — shim the pump bracket until the shaft drops in freely.

hints.title: Free-spin test
hints.message: After torquing the pump bolts, grab the coupling and spin it by hand. It should turn smoothly with no tight spots. Binding means misalignment — loosen and adjust.

hints.title: Reservoir placement height
hints.message: Mount the reservoir so the suction port is above the pump inlet. This gravity-feeds oil to the pump and prevents cavitation on startup.

hints.title: Pressure hose routing
hints.message: Route hoses away from the engine exhaust, fan belt, and any moving parts. Maintain at least a 4-inch bend radius — tight bends weaken the braided reinforcement.

hints.title: Return line routing
hints.message: The return line is lower pressure but higher flow. Use the full 1" ID hose — don't reduce the size. Route it below the reservoir return port so oil flows downhill.

hints.title: Cooler placement
hints.message: Mount the cooler where it gets maximum airflow — the front face of the frame is ideal. Leave at least 2 inches between the cooler and any obstruction.

hints.title: Hydraulic injection hazard
hints.message: A pinhole leak at 2500 PSI can inject fluid through skin, causing severe tissue damage. Never run your hand along a pressurized hose to check for leaks — use cardboard instead.

targets.name: Final Inspection Point
targets.description: Central reference for the final squareness and plumb inspection.

targets.name: Mounting Plate Cut Point
targets.description: Cutting point for the engine mounting plate.

targets.name: Mounting Plate Position
targets.description: Where the mounting plate sits across the frame top.

targets.name: Plate Weld Front-Left
targets.description: Front-left weld point for the mounting plate.

targets.name: Plate Weld Front-Right
targets.description: Front-right weld point for the mounting plate.

targets.name: Plate Weld Back-Left
targets.description: Back-left weld point for the mounting plate.

targets.name: Plate Weld Back-Right
targets.description: Back-right weld point for the mounting plate.

targets.name: Engine Position
targets.description: Where the engine sits on the mounting plate.

targets.name: Engine Bolt Point
targets.description: Torque point for engine mounting bolts.

targets.name: Coupling Position
targets.description: Where the spider coupling mounts on the engine shaft.

targets.name: Hydraulic Pump Position
targets.description: Where the pump mounts, aligned to the coupling.

targets.name: Pump Bolt Point
targets.description: Torque point for pump mounting bolts.

targets.name: Reservoir Position
targets.description: Where the reservoir mounts on the frame side.

targets.name: Reservoir Bolt Point
targets.description: Torque point for reservoir mounting straps.

targets.name: Pressure Hose Route
targets.description: Routing path for the high-pressure hose from pump to manifold.

targets.name: Return Hose Route
targets.description: Routing path for the return hose from manifold to reservoir.

targets.name: Oil Cooler Position
targets.description: Where the oil cooler mounts on the front of the frame.

targets.name: Fitting Tightening Check Point
targets.description: Central reference point for final fitting tightening and system check.
